name: Ring of the Central Mountain of Asturias
name_original: Anillo de la Montaña Central Asturiana
region_intro: Touring cycling around Ribera De Arriba, located in the Central Mountains of Asturias, features a diverse landscape of green mountain pastures, narrow valleys, and leafy beech and oak forests. The region is characterized by its extensive valley alongside the Nalón River, providing scenic routes that often follow river courses. The Vía Verde del Fuso, a dedicated greenway, offers a comfortable cycling experience through varied natural and urban environments. Best touring cycling routes around Ribera De Arriba The most popular touring cycling route is Ring of the Central Mountain of Asturias, a 41.8 miles (67.2 km) difficult trail that takes about 5 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route traverses significant mountainous terrain within the Asturian central range. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Parcoscuro Greenway – Manjoya Route loop from Palombar, a moderate 21.6 miles (34.7 km) path. This route incorporates sections of the Vía Verde del Fuso, offering a blend of natural scenery and accessible cycling. Local touring cyclists also love the Valdemurrio Reservoir – Medieval Tower of Proaza loop from Parteayer, a 56.3 miles (90.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including a reservoir and historical sites, often completed in about 6 hours 36 minutes. Touring cycling around Ribera De Arriba is defined by green mountain pastures, narrow valleys, and river courses. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate greenways to challenging mountain routes. The routes in Ribera De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 290 reviews. More than 6000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ribera De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Ribera De Arriba offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 240 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Yes, for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride, there are several easy routes available. The region's Vía Verde del Fuso is particularly well-suited for a comfortable and enjoyable pedaling experience through pleasant and safe environments, often incorporating sections of greenways.

Q: Can I find family-friendly cycling routes in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Ribera De Arriba's greenways, such as sections of the Vía Verde del Fuso, are excellent choices for family-friendly cycling. These paths offer a safe and scenic environment away from heavy traffic, perfect for cyclists of all ages to enjoy the natural beauty of the region.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Ribera De Arriba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parcoscuro Greenway – Manjoya Route loop from Palombar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 21.6-mile ride blending natural scenery with accessible cycling.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes in Ribera De Arriba often pass through diverse landscapes including green mountain pastures, narrow valleys, and leafy beech and oak forests. You can also encounter significant natural features like the Nalón River and the Valdemurrio Reservoir. Notable landmarks and viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5774152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanga Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peñona&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument (Monte Naranco)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is it possible to access cycling routes in Ribera De Arriba using public transport?
A: While specific public transport options directly to trailheads can vary, Ribera De Arriba's cycling routes, particularly those utilizing the Vía Verde del Fuso, offer convenient access to Oviedo, the capital of Asturias. This connection allows for integration of urban exploration with natural scenery, and public transport to Oviedo is generally well-established.

Q: Where can I find parking for cycling trails in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes that connect urban areas like Soto de Ribera or Posada de Llanera, you can often find parking within or near these towns. For more remote starting points, it's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Ribera De Arriba?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Ribera De Arriba is generally during the spring and autumn months. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, lush green landscapes, and avoid the peak heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. The region's varied terrain and good road surfaces make it enjoyable for much of the year.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs near the cycling routes in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Yes, towns like Soto de Ribera and Posada de Llanera, which are often starting or passing points for cycling routes, offer various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. These provide convenient spots for refreshments, meals, or an overnight stay during your cycling tour.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the cycling trails in Ribera De Arriba?
A: Many outdoor trails in Ribera De Arriba, especially those in natural areas, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to check specific regulations for any particular route or natural park you plan to visit.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Ribera De Arriba?
A: The touring cycling experience in Ribera De Arriba is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 290 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from green mountain pastures to river valleys, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there options for more advanced or long-distance touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Ribera De Arriba, nestled in the Central Mountains of Asturias, offers challenging routes for experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029985885/ring-of-the-central-mountain-of-asturias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ring of the Central Mountain of Asturias&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 41.8-mile trail with significant elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40321629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemurrio Reservoir – Medieval Tower of Proaza loop from Parteayer&lt;/a&gt; spans over 56 miles through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that incorporate historical elements?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. The ancient 'Route of the Silver' (Ruta de la Plata), Roman in origin and historically used for commerce, crosses the territory of Ribera De Arriba, offering a historical dimension to some cycling tours. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1070128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Mary of Naranco&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1070132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel de Lillo&lt;/a&gt; near some routes.
tips: A beautiful section along a paved track that passes through one of the tails of the Alfilorios reservoir, which is located in the course of the Barrea river. The conjunction of the views of the peaks and the deep green vegetation with the waters of the reservoir creates a unique landscape.

On this beautiful stretch, part of the dreaded L'Angliru climb, you'll have to take it easy to overcome the gradient, which in some cases exceeds 16%. The views of the surrounding landscape are breathtaking.

Old copper mines that are being rehabilitated little by little. It has a very original and precious viewpoint.
The access track from Llamo is mostly made of concrete and with very hard slopes.
Suitable for Gravel, Hybrid and Mtb bikes.

A beautiful town with a rich history and culture. Here are some things you can do:
Visit the Church of San Juan Bautista with a unique combination of Romanesque and Gothic architecture.
Explore the Caudal River: enjoy the impressive natural landscape.
Discover the history of Mieres at the Museum of Mining and Industry
Visit the Valdecarzana Palace – This palace dates back to the 17th century and features a unique mix of architectural styles, including Renaissance and Baroque.
Try local cuisine such as Asturian fabada (a bean stew), cachopo (a stuffed meat dish) and cider.
Relax at the Las Caldas Thermal Spa, which features natural hot springs and a variety of spa treatments.

The Alto del Padrún is located in one of the classic seesaws of the roads that run through the mining basin between Mieres and Oviedo. It is on a stretch of road with moderate traffic, but one that can be easily traveled.
Olloniego is a picturesque Asturian town in the mining basin. It preserves some traditional houses and some manor houses. It is an ideal place for cycling to take a break in any of its bars.
